1: And the child Samuel ministered unto the LORD LORD before Eli. And the word of the LORD LORD was precious in those days; there was there was no open vision.
2: And it came to pass at that time, when Eli was was laid down in his place, and his eyes began to wax dim, that that he could not see;
3: and ere the lamp of God went out in the temple of the LORD LORD , where the ark of God was was , and Samuel was laid down to sleep to sleep ;
4: that the LORD LORD called Samuel: and he answered, Here am am I.
5: And he ran unto Eli, and said, Here am am I; for thou calledst me. And he said, I called not; lie down again. And he went and lay down.
6: And the LORD LORD called yet again, Samuel. And Samuel arose and went to Eli, and said, Here am am I; for thou didst call me. And he answered, I called not, my son; lie down again.
7: Now Samuel did not yet know the LORD LORD , neither was the word of the LORD LORD yet revealed unto him.
8: And the LORD LORD called Samuel again the third time. And he arose and went to Eli, and said, Here am am I; for thou didst call me. And Eli perceived that the LORD LORD had called the child.
9: Therefore Eli said unto Samuel, Go, lie down: and it shall be, if he call thee, that thou shalt say, Speak, LORD LORD ; for thy servant heareth. So Samuel went and lay down in his place.
10: And the LORD LORD came, and stood, and called as at other times, Samuel, Samuel. Then Samuel answered, Speak; for thy servant heareth.
11: And the LORD LORD said to Samuel, Behold, I will do a thing in Israel, at which both the ears of every one that heareth it shall tingle.
12: In that day I will perform against Eli all things things which I have spoken concerning his house: when I begin, I will also make an end.
13: For I have told him that I will judge his house for ever for the iniquity which he knoweth; because his sons made themselves vile, and he restrained them not.
14: And therefore I have sworn unto the house of Eli, that the iniquity of Eli's house shall not be purged with sacrifice nor offering for ever.
15: And Samuel lay until the morning, and opened the doors of the house of the LORD LORD . And Samuel feared to shew Eli the vision.
16: Then Eli called Samuel, and said, Samuel, my son. And he answered, Here am am I.
17: And he said, What is is the thing that the LORD the LORD LORD hath said unto thee? I pray thee hide it it not from me: God do so to thee, and more also, if thou hide any any thing from me of all the things that he said unto thee.
18: And Samuel told him every whit, and hid nothing from him. And he said, It is is the LORD LORD : let him do what seemeth him good.
19: And Samuel grew, and the LORD LORD was with him, and did let none of his words fall to the ground.
20: And all Israel from Dan even to Beer-sheba knew that Samuel was was established to be to be a prophet of the LORD LORD .
21: And the LORD LORD appeared again in Shiloh: for the LORD LORD revealed himself to Samuel in Shiloh by the word of the LORD LORD .
